revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art sprite sheet for a 2D action RPG terrain-tile asset.

STRICT OUTPUT CONTRACT:
- Canvas size: exactly 64x64 px.
- Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns, 16 frames total.
- Each frame/cell: exactly 16x16 px.
- No visible grid lines, no borders, no tile IDs, no labels, no text, no watermark, no UI.
- Every tile must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and keep the same scale.
- Crisp SNES / 16-bit-style low-resolution pixel art, sharp readable pixel clusters, no antialiasing blur.
- Opaque tile artwork edge-to-edge where grass exists; fill any truly empty unused pixels with solid #FF00FF magenta only.

PROFILE AND PRESET:
Profile: snes16_platformer_v1.
Preset: world_grass_variation_16.
Asset kind: terrain-tile.
Asset type: tile.
World-map grassland variation tiles, not close-up village grass. Think broad satoyama valley floor regions and low-frequency natural ground tone drift, not repeated square stamps.

VISUAL STYLE:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, cheerful hand-painted Ghibli-like feeling translated into crisp pixel art. Warm golden-hour sunlight from top-left. Base colors: deep emerald and matcha green, subtle warm yellow-green sunlit highlights, cool blue-green shadows beneath grass blades. Consistent palette and scale across all frames. Use one-pixel dark outlines only where useful for small features such as stones, moss, flowers, and susuki stems.

TILEMAP QUALITY:
- Final sheet must not read as a checkerboard when tiled.
- Avoid repeated square stamp motifs and high-frequency checkerboard texture.
- Every cell must be seamless on all four sides and compatible with every other grass cell.
- Fill the four corners of every cell with the same visual density as the center: no sparse corners, no lighter corner halo, no diamond pattern when tiled.
- Vary texture, edge breakup, micro-lighting, and color drift subtly while keeping the set cohesive.
- Grass should include broad color drift, regional patches, and low-frequency texture rather than one repeated grass stamp.

MICRO TEXTURE ADAPTED TO 16x16 CELLS:
Within each 16x16 cell, imply a tiny 4x4 subcell structure using very small clusters of sharply pointed sasa grass blades at varied angles and heights. Do not draw subcell borders. Avoid large blank patches. No single dominant motif should be recognizable when repeated. Scatter elements across different cells and positions: tiny single-petal white or pale-yellow wildflowers, micro trefoil clover clusters, soft moss pads on thin pebbles, occasional darker shadow blade clusters.

STRICT SHEET LAYOUT:
Row 1, cells 1-4: four fully seamless interior lush grass variations, filled edge-to-edge, subtle emerald/matcha drift.
Row 2, cells 5-8: four more seamless lush grass variations with different dominant tiny grass-cluster arrangements, still edge-to-edge.
Row 3, cells 9-12: four seamless variations with mild brightness/hue shifts: one includes a small flower group, one a moss patch, one a darker shadow blade cluster, one a tiny pebble.
Row 4 accent tiles:
- Cell 13: small white flower group on lush seamless grass.
- Cell 14: denser moss-and-stone micro-scene on seamless grass.
- Cell 15: slender striped-stem clump of susuki ornamental grass accent on seamless grass.
- Cell 16: bare golden-earth patch rimmed naturally by grass, transition hint, still no hard rectangular band.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS:
No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, likenesses, or recognizable franchise elements. No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird, companion creature, letter emblem, text, watermark, UI, character, visible grid lines, visible subcell lines, hard tile borders, repeated grass stamp, high-frequency checkerboard, corner-density mismatch, blank corners, antialias blur.
